title: Malty Silky Argentinian Potato Fritter
description: A kid-friendly Argentinian dish made with potato starch, pasta-water-emulsion, and a hint of malt.

ingredients.name: Malt syrup
ingredients.notes: You can substitute with honey or maple syrup if needed.

instructions: In a large bowl, combine potato starch, mashed potatoes, and pasta water. Mix until well combined.
Gradually add the malt syrup and mix until a smooth dough forms.
Add the egg and mix until fully incorporated.
Add the flour, salt, and pepper, and mix until a smooth, shiny dough forms.
Heat the butter in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat.
When the butter is melted and foamy, use a 1/4 cup measuring cup to scoop the dough into the skillet.
Press the dough down with a spatula to form a flat fritter.
Cook for 3-4 minutes on the first side, or until golden brown.
Flip the fritter and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until golden brown on the second side.
Repeat with the remaining dough, greasing the skillet with butter as needed.
